1.gl: to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application
1.kj: accomplish, advance, appoint, apt, be at, become, bear, bestow, bring forth, bruise, be busy, [idiom] certainly, have the charge of, commit, deal (with), deck, [phrase] displease,

1.gl: to be high actively, to rise or raise (in various applications, literally or figuratively)

1.gl: physically, to throw (a stone, an arrow) at or away; especially to revere or worship (with extended hands); intensively, to bemoan (by wringing the hands)
1.dv: a primitive root; used only as denominative from H3027 (יָד); literally, to use (i.e. hold out) the hand;

1.gl: gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a superlative
1.kj: angels, [idiom] exceeding, God (gods) (-dess, -ly), [idiom] (very) great, judges, [idiom] mighty.

1.gl: thou and thee, or (plural) ye and you
1.dv: or (shortened); אַתָּ; or אַת; feminine (irregular) sometimes אַתִּי; plural masculine אַתֶּם; feminine אַתֶּן; or אַתֵּנָהlemma אתֵּנָה missing vowel

1.gl: an appellation, as amark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character
1.dv: a primitive word (perhaps rather from through the idea of definite and conspicuous position; compare H8064 (שָׁמַיִם));

2.gl: to put (used in a great variety of applications, literal, figurative, inferentially, and elliptically)

2.dv: a primitive root; to clip off; specifically (as denominative from H1210 (בָּצִיר))

2.kj: (come from) another (man, place), fanner, go away, (e-) strange(-r, thing, woman).

2.gl: a city (a place guarded by waking or a watch) in the widest sense (even of a mere encampment or post)

9.kj: angels, [idiom] exceeding, God (gods) (-dess, -ly), [idiom] (very) great, judges, [idiom] mighty.

12.gl: properly, to touch, i.e. lay the hand upon (for any purpose; euphemistically, to lie with a woman); by implication, to reach (figuratively, to arrive, acquire); violently, to strike (punish, defeat, destroy, etc.)
12.kj: beat, ([idiom] be able to) bring (down), cast, come (nigh), draw near (nigh), get up, happen, join, near, plague, reach (up), smite, strike, touch.

12.kj: abase, bring (cast, put) down, debase, humble (self), be (bring, lay, make, put) low(-er).
